summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording captures a high‑energy 11/14/78 show highlighted by scorching Sugaree, monster Bertha and inspired Keith on keys.
review: The audience tape is remarkably open and detailed – you can hear each instrument clearly despite the enthusiastic crowd.  The performance itself is electric from start to finish, with standout versions of Sugaree, Bertha, Wharf Rat and a host of deep‑cut gems that keep the show flowing with relentless momentum.

ai_rating.rationale: All detailed reviews praise both the musical performance and the audio fidelity; only one irrelevant low‑star comment exists, so the combined show (70%) + recording (30%) rating is well above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Open‑mic audience capture gives a spacious, immersive feel; near‑crystal‑clear separation of instruments even with crowd noise, making the tape sound as if you were there.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert maintains frenetic energy from the opening Sugaree through a powerful second half; transitions are tight and the pacing never lags, delivering constant momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note his inspired solos – especially on Sugaree, Stagger Lee and other jams – calling his playing “smoking” and “elite.”
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard work is highlighted as aggressive, front‑and‑center, with tasteful liftoffs that elevate the entire set.
